'''Paul Carr''' (born 13 May 1967) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s. He played at representative level for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]], and at club level for [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] and [[Sheffield Eagles]], as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|lk}} forward.<ref>{{cite book| first1= Graham| last1= Williams| first2= Peter| last2= Lush| first3= David| last3= Farrar| title= The British Rugby League Records Book| publisher= London League| isbn= 978-1-903659-49-6| year= 2009| pages= 106–107}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Paul Carr played {{rlp|sr}} in [[Sheffield Eagles]]' 17-8 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1998 Challenge Cup|1998]] [[Challenge Cup]] Final during [[Super League III]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 2 May 1998.<ref>{{cite web |title=Sheffield beat the odds to trounce Wigan |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/sport/rugby_league/87046.stm |website=BBC News |access-date=14 July 2025 |date=2 May 1998}}</ref>

Carr was inducted into Sheffield Eagles Hall of Fame in 2022.<ref>{{cite web |title=Paul Carr |url=https://heritage.sheffieldeagles.com/players/Paul-Carr-150 |website=Sheffield Eagles Heritage Project |publisher=The Eagles Foundation |access-date=14 July 2025}}</ref>

===International honours===
Paul Carr won a [[Cap (sport)|cap]] for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] while at Sheffield Eagles in 1997 against France.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/paul-carr/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref>
